analysis_summary.title: Entity Flag Handlers Analysis
analysis_summary.description: Reverse engineering analysis of SetEntityFlagHandler, DisallowInvisibleHandler, and AttachObjectHandler from Menace.Tactical.Skills.Effects namespace

base_handler_layout.description: All handlers inherit from SkillEventHandler base class

0x00-0x0F.name: il2cpp_object_header
0x00-0x0F.description: Standard Unity IL2CPP object header (class pointer, GC info)

0x10.name: Skill
0x10.description: Reference to the skill instance that owns this handler

0x18.name: Template
0x18.description: Reference to the effect template (ScriptableObject data asset)

OnAdded.description: Called when the effect is applied to an entity. If EntityFlags equals 0x800 (FLAG_SHOW_HUD), retrieves the entity, checks if it's a Structure type, and shows the StructureHUD.

OnUpdate.description: Called every update tick. ORs the template's EntityFlags (offset 0x58) into the target entity's CombatFlags (Entity+0xEC), applying the flag effect continuously.

0x58.name: EntityFlags
0x58.description: Bitmask of entity flags to apply. Known values: 0x800 = FLAG_SHOW_HUD (shows structure health/status HUD)

known_values.0x800: FLAG_SHOW_HUD - Triggers StructureHUD.Show() for Structure entities

Entity+0xEC.name: CombatFlags
Entity+0xEC.description: Entity combat flags bitmask, modified by OR operation with template flags

OnVerifyTarget.description: Target verification callback. Prevents targeting invisible entities unless player has direct line of sight. Returns 1 (allow) or 0 (disallow).

template_fields.note: No custom fields at 0x58+. This effect uses base class fields only.

known_bits.0x08: Player has direct line of sight to this tile

targeting_logic.description: Returns allow (1) if: (a) entity is not player-controlled, OR (b) player-controlled but does NOT have LOS (bit 0x8 clear), OR (c) target entity is not invisible (state != 2). Returns disallow (0) if player-controlled with LOS and target is invisible.

OnAdded.description: Called when effect is applied. Instantiates the prefab at the entity's spawnpoint transform. Uses PlayerSpawnpointIndex or EnemySpawnpointIndex based on entity faction.

OnRemoved.description: Called when effect is removed. Destroys the instantiated object. If object has ParticleSystem, stops it gracefully before delayed destroy.

0x58.name: Prefab
0x58.description: Reference to the prefab/GameObject to instantiate and attach to the entity

0x60.name: PlayerSpawnpointIndex
0x60.description: Index into element spawnpoint list for player-controlled entities. 0 = no specific spawnpoint.

0x64.name: EnemySpawnpointIndex
0x64.description: Index into element spawnpoint list for enemy/AI-controlled entities. 0 = no specific spawnpoint.

0x20.name: InstantiatedObject
0x20.description: Runtime reference to the instantiated prefab instance. Set in OnAdded, cleared in OnRemoved.

SpawnpointNames.description: Static string array initialized in .cctor with 4 spawnpoint identifiers

ghidra_comments_added.content: SetEntityFlagHandler.OnAdded: Handler structure and FLAG_SHOW_HUD logic
SetEntityFlagHandler.OnUpdate: OR operation for flag application
Tile+0x1C: LOS flags (bit 3 = 0x8 = player has direct LOS)
AttachObjectHandler.OnAdded: Full handler and template field layout
Spawnpoint index selection based on entity faction
AttachObjectHandler.OnRemoved: Cleanup logic with ParticleSystem handling

additional_notes: All handlers follow the factory pattern: EffectTemplate.Create() instantiates the handler and sets Handler+0x18 to the template reference
Handler+0x10 (BaseSkill*) is set by the skill system when the handler is registered
Template offsets start at 0x58 because 0x00-0x57 is occupied by ScriptableObject/BaseEffect base class fields
The comparison AddItemSlotHandler also uses 0x58 (ItemSlot enum) and 0x5c (SlotCount), confirming the 0x58 offset pattern
The comparison AddSkillHandler uses 0x58 as event type enum (1 = WhenAdded), further confirming pattern
